transcript:
HCP: Hi Sarah, thanks for coming in today
HCP: so you're here for a bit of a review on your knee and the back pain you mentioned last week
HCP: you've also been feeling really tired lately, haven't you?
HCP: yeah, I've been dragging myself through everything, just exhausted all day
HCP: well, let's start with that knee, it sounds like you've been having some swelling and clicking
HCP: yes, it's been swelling up every time I walk the dog, and it clicks when I squat
HCP: we need to get this looked at properly, maybe an orthopaedic review would help
HCP: now, moving on to your back, you said you have lower back pain that's been going on for months
HCP: right, it's worse when I sit at the computer, and I can't get out of bed in the mornings
HCP: okay, that sounds like mechanical back pain, we'll need a physio to sort that out
HCP: and finally, this fatigue, you've been sleeping okay but you're still wiped out
HCP: yeah, even after a full night's sleep I feel like I haven't moved at all
HCP: let's check your bloods to rule out anemia or thyroid issues, just to be safe
HCP: sure, that would be great, I'm worried it might be something serious
HCP: don't worry, it's probably just vitamin deficiency or something minor
HCP: so just to recap, we're sending you to Orthopaedics for your knee
HCP: and to Physiotherapy for your back pain
HCP: and we'll run some blood tests to sort out the fatigue
HCP: sounds like a plan, thanks for walking me through it
HCP: no worries, feel better soon Sarah

reason.text: knee swelling and clicking, lower back pain, fatigue
reason.evidence_span: knee and the back pain you mentioned last week

request.text: Orthopaedics for knee, Physiotherapy for back, blood tests for fatigue
request.evidence_span: maybe an orthopaedic review would help

transcript:
HCP: Hi, I'm Dr Evans. Thanks for coming in today.
HCP: So, how can I help you?
HCP: It sounds like your knee is bothering you again.
HCP: Can you tell me a bit more about that pain?
Patient: Yeah, it's been really sore since I started running.
HCP: When exactly did this start?
Patient: About three weeks ago, after my long run.
HCP: Does it hurt when you walk normally?
Patient: Just a little bit, but mostly when I go up stairs.
HCP: Okay, so it's worse with activity.
Patient: Exactly, and it swells up a bit too.
HCP: Let's look at your history then.
HCP: Any previous knee injuries?
Patient: Not really, just some minor sprains in high school.
HCP: And any family history of knee problems?
Patient: My dad had to replace his knee at fifty.
HCP: Right, that's relevant.
HCP: How is your weight?
Patient: I think I'm a bit heavy for my age.
HCP: Has that changed recently?
Patient: Not really, maybe a kilo or two.
HCP: Okay, let's check your meds.
HCP: Are you on any regular painkillers?
Patient: Just panadol when it's really bad.
HCP: And no other medications?
Patient: No, I don't take any others.
HCP: Good, so we're clear on that.
HCP: Any allergies?
Patient: No known allergies.
HCP: Perfect.
HCP: Based on this, I want to refer you to Orthopaedics.
HCP: They can assess your knee properly.
HCP: They'll probably do an X-ray or MRI.
HCP: Please go and see them for a review.

reason.text: knee pain, worse with activity and swelling
reason.evidence_span: It sounds like your knee is bothering you again

history.evidence_span: just some minor sprains in high school
My dad had to replace his knee at fifty

current_meds.evidence_span: Just panadol when it's really bad

request.evidence_span: They can assess your knee properly

transcript:
HCP: hi there, how are you feeling today?
HCP: so, what brings you in to the clinic right now?
Patient: well, basically I just need a bit of help with my knee pain
HCP: right, I see, and where exactly does it hurt?
Patient: it's right in the front of my knee, especially when I go down stairs
HCP: okay, and how long has this been going on for you?
Patient: it's been a few months now, started after I played tennis a couple of weeks ago
HCP: right, and has it gotten any worse since then?
Patient: yeah, actually it's getting worse every single day
HCP: I'm sorry to hear that, have you tried any treatments so far?
Patient: I've been taking panadol but it really doesn't help much
HCP: okay, and do you have any other medical conditions we need to know about?
Patient: not really, just some mild asthma which I manage with ventolin
HCP: right, and when was the last time you had a checkup?
Patient: probably about a year ago, nothing major happened then
HCP: okay, thanks for telling me all that
HCP: I think you need to see a specialist to get this sorted out
HCP: I'll write you a referral to Orthopaedics for you
HCP: they'll be able to do a proper exam and maybe order some scans
HCP: okay, anything else you want to ask me before we go?
Patient: no, that's all, thanks so much for your help
HCP: absolutely, take care and I'll send that referral off now

reason.text: knee pain in front of knee, worse on stairs
reason.evidence_span: it's right in the front of my knee, especially when I go down stairs

request.text: proper exam and maybe order some scans
request.evidence_span: they'll be able to do a proper exam and maybe order some scans

transcript:
HCP: hi there, thanks for coming in today
HCP: how are you feeling, Sarah?
Patient: a bit tired honestly, and my knees are acting up
HCP: yeah I can see that from the way you're walking
HCP: so let's talk about the main thing bothering you today
Patient: it's definitely the knee pain, right?
HCP: absolutely, sounds like osteoarthritis is flaring up
Patient: yeah, it's really stiff in the morning
HCP: how long has that stiffness been going on?
Patient: probably about two months now
HCP: okay, and does it hurt more with activity?
Patient: yes, climbing stairs is a nightmare lately
HCP: right, and do you have any swelling?
Patient: a bit, especially after a long day
HCP: got it, let's look at your background
HCP: do you have any other chronic conditions?
Patient: just hypertension and a bit of high cholesterol
HCP: okay, so what meds are you taking for those?
Patient: lisinopril for the blood pressure and atorvastatin
HCP: got it, and anything else for the knee?
Patient: I've been using ice packs but nothing else
HCP: no known allergies to medications?
Patient: nope, never had a bad reaction
HCP: perfect, so here's what I think we should do
HCP: I'm referring you to Rheumatology for a closer look
HCP: they can do some imaging and maybe adjust your treatment
Patient: thanks, I appreciate the help
HCP: no problem, we'll get that referral letter sorted out

transcript:
HCP: hi there, how can I help you today?
HCP: so you're here for a bit of a tangle with your knee and your skin?
Patient: yeah, basically my knee's been throbbing for ages and then the dermatitis on my arm just flared up yesterday
HCP: right, let's sort that knee first, tell me what's happening there exactly?
Patient: well it's really swollen whenever I walk my dog and it hurts to bend it
HCP: okay, sounds like an orthopaedic issue, maybe a physio first but let's get that specialist involved
HCP: and for the skin, is it itchy or just red and raw?
Patient: it's so itchy and it's oozing a bit, I've been using calamine but nothing works
HCP: right, so we need a Dermatology referral for that rash and the knee needs Orthopaedics
HCP: regarding your knee, any history of injury or just wear and tear?
Patient: mostly just wear and tear, I play netball on the weekend which is making it worse
HCP: got it, and for the skin, are you taking any steroids or antibiotics?
Patient: no, I'm only on some panadol for the pain and nothing else for the rash
HCP: okay, let's make sure we have that down
HCP: any other meds you're on regularly?
Patient: just the panadol and some vitamin D supplements
HCP: great, no known allergies to mention either
HCP: so the summary is knee pain from netball and itchy oozing rash
HCP: I'll send you off to Orthopaedics for the knee and Dermatology for the skin
HCP: please review and advise on management for both conditions

specialty.evidence_span: Orthopaedics for the knee and Dermatology for the skin

transcript:
HCP: hi there, it's a bit of a long time since we last spoke
HCP: how have you been feeling lately?
Patient: well, honestly, just terrible to be honest with you
HCP: what's going on then?
Patient: my knee's been acting up for ages now
HCP: okay, so what's the problem with your knee specifically?
Patient: it's really swollen and stiff, especially when I try to walk
HCP: has it been hurting a lot?
Patient: yeah, it's quite painful when it rains too
HCP: got any history of arthritis or joint issues before?
Patient: yeah, I've had a few bad knees in the past
HCP: what are you taking for the pain right now?
Patient: just ibuprofen and some paracetamol
HCP: any other medicines or supplements?
Patient: not really, just that and some vitamin D
HCP: no known allergies I suppose
Patient: correct, no allergies at all
HCP: so I think a referral to Orthopaedics would be best for you
HCP: please review and assess the knee joint for possible surgery

reason.text: swollen stiff knee painful when walking
reason.evidence_span: it's really swollen and stiff, especially when I try to walk

request.text: review and assess knee for surgery
request.evidence_span: please review and assess the knee joint for possible surgery
